searchDocs.title: setCustomHeadOffset​
searchDocs.content: </> HeadSystem.setCustomHeadOffset(headOffset: number?) → () Sets a custom offset for the head. By default the system can handle R6 and R15 rigs automatically however when using custom rigs then you can use this function to set a custom offset. If headoffset defualt behaviour is restored.

searchDocs.title: setBounds​
searchDocs.content: </> HeadSystem.setBounds( lower: number?, upper: number? ) → () Sets the clamping values of how far the head can look up and down. Set to nil for default behaviour.

searchDocs.title: calculateLookAngles​
searchDocs.content: </> HeadSystem.calculateLookAngles( cameraCFrame: CFrame , rootCFrame: CFrame ) → ( number, number ) Calculates the head look angles from the given cameraCFrame in relation to the rootCFrame.

searchDocs.title: updateLookAngles​
searchDocs.content: </> HeadSystem.updateLookAngles( character: Model , yAngle: number, xAngle: number ) → () Updates the head rotation for the character, the yAngle and xAngle should be normalized and everything before being passed into this function.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> BaseDispatcher.new() → BaseDispatcher Create a new dispatcher. This should NOT be called directly on the base dispatcher unless you are creating your own dispatcher.

searchDocs.title: is​
searchDocs.content: </> BaseDispatcher.is(obj: T) → bool Check if the passed obj is a dispatcher, this can be either the BaseDispatcher or any class which extends BaseDispatcher.

searchDocs.title: collectFunctions​
searchDocs.content: </> BaseDispatcher:collectFunctions(name: string) → {() → ()} Returns all function on services which have the passed name.

searchDocs.title: dispatch​
searchDocs.content: </> BaseDispatcher:dispatch( name: string, ...: any ) → () This is the method which is called by a lifecycle to actually call all the functions.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Lifecycle.new( name: string, dispatcher: BaseDispatcher ) → () Create a new Lifecycle with the given name. You can optionally pass a dispatcher to use, it defaults to an AsyncDispatcher if nothing is passed.

searchDocs.title: fire​
searchDocs.content: </> Lifecycle:fire(...: any) → () Fires the lifecycle, anything passed into ... will also be passed to every function which this lifecycle invokes.

searchDocs.title: updateSoundForMaterial​
searchDocs.content: </> FootStepSystem.updateSoundForMaterial( material: Material , sound: string? ) → () Sets the sound for the passed material to be sound where if sound is nil then the fallback sound is used, this by default is the normal roblox walking sound however this can be overrided with LifelikeCharactersServiceClient.changeFallbackSound.

searchDocs.title: changeFallbackSound​
searchDocs.content: </> FootStepSystem.changeFallbackSound(sound: string) → () Change the default footstep sound that is used when a Material doesnt have a sound set.

searchDocs.title: setupFootsteps​
searchDocs.content: </> FootStepSystem.setupFootsteps() → () Connects the needed events.

searchDocs.title: setHeadRotationReplicationRate​
searchDocs.content: </> LifelikeCharactersServiceClient.setHeadRotationReplicationRate(rate: number) → () Override the head replication rate, by default the rate is 1/20. WARNING You shouldn't mess with this too much as higher values can cause bandwidth issues.

searchDocs.title: setFirstPersonOffset​
searchDocs.content: </> LifelikeCharactersServiceClient.setFirstPersonOffset(offset: number) → () Sets how far farward the camera will be in first person compared to normal. Note that the negative scale is farward relative to the head.

searchDocs.content: </> LifelikeCharactersServiceClient.changeFallbackSound(sound: string) → () Change the default footstep sound that is used when a Material doesnt have a sound set.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Registry.new( parent: Instance? , validationFunction: (obj: any) → boolean?, processFunction: (obj: Instance ) → T? ) → () Create a new registry. The parent paramater if passed will just call Registry.loadFromParent, pass nil to skip this. You can pass a validationFunction which gets asserted on every object to ensure its the correct type. Finally a processFunction can be passed which gets run on every object and can convert objects into something else, for example you could create a processFunction like this: Registry.new(script.Parent.ItemModels, nil, function(instance: Model) local item = {} for k, v in instance:GetAttributes() do items[k] = v end item.model = instance return item end) INFO If your data is stored as module scripts this will automatically pick that up and require the module scripts for you. However if you have a folder with mixed instance (e.g. ModuleScripts and Folders) if will not work properly and require the module scripts but add the raw instacne folders themselves (if no validationFunction is present).

searchDocs.title: add​
searchDocs.content: </> Registry:add( name: string, obj: T ) → () Adds something to the registry.

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"Object did not pass validation function"	The `obj` passed doesnt pass the `validationFunction` after the `processFunction`

searchDocs.title: loadFromParent​
searchDocs.content: </> Registry:loadFromParent(instance: Instance ) → () Load all the instacnes from the given instance. Note that this only calls :GetChildren() on the instance.

searchDocs.title: doesExist​
searchDocs.content: </> Registry:doesExist(name: string) → boolean Check if an item with the name name exists in the repository.

searchDocs.title: getItem​
searchDocs.content: </> Registry:getItem(name: string) → T Gets an item with the name name from the registry, if it doesn't exist this function will error.

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"Object with the name "{name}" doesn't exist in the repository."	No object with the name `name` exist in the repository. You should use "doesExist" if you are cannot guarantee if an object will exist.

searchDocs.title: Dispatchers​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Sapling.Dispatchers: any All the built-in dispatchers lifecycle dispatchers.

searchDocs.title: loadServices​
searchDocs.content: </> Sapling.loadServices( parent: Instance , recursive: boolean? ) → () Loads all module scripts under parent ignoring anything which isn't a ModuleScript instance.

searchDocs.title: loadPackages​
searchDocs.content: </> Sapling.loadPackages(packagesFolder: Folder ) → () This function only needs to be called if you are using any other Sapling Framework packages, simply point it to your Packages folder and Sapling will handle the rest. If you do not call this function at the right time, or at all, pacakges will throw an error and tell you that you have not called this function. WARNING This function NEEDS to be called before you call loadServices otherwise packages will error. DANGER If you use Actor you will need to call this in every Actor to avoid errors.

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"You can only call loadPackages one time"	You should only call this function once, before you call `loadService`.

searchDocs.title: fireLifecycle​
searchDocs.content: </> Sapling.fireLifecycle( name: string, ...: any ) → () Utility function to quickly fire a lifecycle, this is so you dont need to create a lifecycle object to fire it and whatever. You can just call this function with the name and any paramaters you want to pass.

searchDocs.title: createLifecycle​
searchDocs.content: </> Sapling.createLifecycle( name: string, dispatcher: BaseDispatcher ) → Lifecycle Just calls Lifecycle.new

searchDocs.title: extendBaseDispatcher​
searchDocs.content: </> Sapling.extendBaseDispatcher() → () This function returns a new class that extends the BaseDispatcher. Here is a basic example of a blocking dispatcher: local BlockingDispatcher = Sapling.extendBaseDispatcher() function BlockingDispatcher.dispatch( self: typeof(BlockingDispatcher), name: string, ...: any ) -- The :collectFunctions() is a method on the baseDispatcher class, you can override it yourself -- if you really want but there is simply no point to. local functions = self:collectFunctions(name) -- Loop through all the functiosn and call them for _, fn in functions do fn(...) end end return BlockingDispatcher

searchDocs.title: Service​
searchDocs.content: </> type Service = {[string | unknown]: unknown}

searchDocs.title: InjectedAPI​
searchDocs.content: </> interface InjectedAPI { addModule: (module: ModuleScript ) → () } This is the type which gets injected into _G. This allows packages to just be loaded with Sapling.loadPackages without manually calling require on every single pacakge you use in your game.

searchDocs.title: addService​
searchDocs.content: </> ServiceManager.addService( module: ModuleScript , service: Service? ) → () Adds a service, you can either pass just the module script itself and it will require it for you using safeRequire and throws a custom, better error message.

searchDocs.title: collectFunctions​
searchDocs.content: </> ServiceManager.collectFunctions(name: string) → {(...any) → ()} Gets every function in the service which has the name name.

searchDocs.title: Sapling Framework
searchDocs.content: Sapling Framework These docs mostly explain theory stuff for the entire framework. If you are looking for the docs for a specific pacakge, its all documented within ints API page.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> QueryBuilder.new() → ()

searchDocs.title: withTag​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:withTag(tag: string) → QueryBuilder Adds the tag to the search query.

searchDocs.title: either​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:either(tag: string) → QueryBuilder An or operation between whats on the left to the right. For example: :withTag("Apple"):either():withTag("Orange"):hasAttribute("IsPeeled") -- Has the tag "Apple" OR Has the tag "Orange" with an attribute of "IsPeeled"

searchDocs.title: child​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:child() → QueryBuilder Anything after this method is called will act on children of queried instances that match the previous cases.

searchDocs.title: descendant​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:descendant() → QueryBuilder Same as QueryBuilder.child but for descendant.

searchDocs.title: negate​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:negate() → QueryBuilder Negates the next function that is aclled. For example: :negate():withTag("cool") -- Does NOT have the tag "cool"

searchDocs.title: hasAttribute​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:hasAttribute(attributeName: string) → QueryBuilder Anything which has the attributeName set to anything but nil

searchDocs.title: hasAttribute​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:hasAttribute( attributeName: string, attributeValue: string | any ) → QueryBuilder Anything which has the attributeName set to attributeValue. See more info about how attributeValue works: QueryBuilder.wherePropertyIs

searchDocs.title: whichIsA​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:whichIsA(class: string) → QueryBuilder Anything which is a of class class (:IsA())

searchDocs.title: named​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:named(name: string) → QueryBuilder Anything which has the name name.

searchDocs.title: wherePropertyIs​
searchDocs.content: returns-self </> QueryBuilder:wherePropertyIs( property: string, value: string | any ) → QueryBuilder Anything which has the its property set to value. For best results you should pass the value as a string, for example: :wherePropertyIs("CanCollide", "false") WARNING If you pass a normal value into value then tostring(value) will be called on the value.

searchDocs.title: query​
searchDocs.content: </> QueryBuilder:query() → string Returns the built up query string.

searchDocs.title: Packages​
searchDocs.content: Firstly, there are 2 types of sapling packages:  Services: A singleton which works as a normal service however is handled fully by Sapling, including lifecycle.Standalone: A standalone module that works by itself and exports all its functionality with a class or a module.  It doesn't really matter too much if you understand the differences between these. However what you do need to know is that for Sapling provided services to work you must call loadPackages pointing it to your packages folder before you load your services.  As an example here is a typical main server script:  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local Sapling = required(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.Sapling) Sapling.loadPackages(ReplicatedStorage.Packages) Sapling.loadServices(script.Parent.Services) Sapling.fireLifecycle("OnStart")

searchDocs.title: Specifing Dependencies​
searchDocs.content: info In the future I might make a little CLI tool to aid with dependencies.  First you need to decide which version of Sapling you want to use, you shouldn't really update Major version of Sapling as they will bring breaking changes. Furthermoer all packages must be on the same version to work fully.  Here is an example of consuming the core sapling package:  [dependencies] Sapling = { repo = "its-a-bit-random/Sapling", rev = "v1.0.0", path = "packages/sapling" }
